nodes.title: Sustain Dev Policy & Gov
nodes.description: History, evolution and foundations of sustainable development policy at multiple levels of governance, ranging from the United Nations to local communities/cities. Learn about analyzing/evaluating wide range of sustainable development policies. Emphasis on understanding complex system dynamics modeling and adaptive management approaches to address sustainable development challenges.
nodes.other_requirements: Restricted to PhD with Masters by permission only

nodes.title: Sustainable Dev PEG Doc Sem
nodes.description: Focus will rotate among three main themes: project resource development skills and techniques (e.g. grant writing and management); stakeholder engagement; and dissertation proposal preparation. Prerequisite: Sustainable Development Policy, Economics, & Governance Doctoral student.
nodes.other_requirements: Sustainable Development Policy, Economics, & Governance Doctoral student. Graduate standing only; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: CL:Sustainable Community Dev
nodes.description: Introduction to perspectives and methods used to develop healthy communities that are economically, socially, and environmentally sustainable with rural and urban, US and international examples. Prerequisites: CDAE 1020, ENVS 1510, or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: ENVS 2470.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Sustainable Fashion
nodes.description: Expands understanding of the fashion production processes  through an environmental and social lens. Students will apply innovation theories to critically explore  transdisciplinary sustainable practices, on all levels of the fashion industry. Through local and global economies, a vast perspective will create deeper sustainable fashion innovation.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Applied Computer Graphics
nodes.description: Directed research, planning, design, technical experimentation, production, and evaluation for computer-generated design application. Prerequisites: CDAE 2160, CDAE 2600, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Prereqs enforced by the system: CDAE 2160 or 2600; Open to PCOM Majors and Applied Design Minors only, or instructor permission. Open to degree and PACE students. Experience using the Adobe Suite is highly recommended. This course is a studio-based class - including short lectures, design demos and discussion. Required materials for this course are a subscription to the Adobe Suite and access to printers on campus. Recommended prerequisite: CDAE 1160 or CDAE 1170 or experience using Adobe Suite in other graphic design courses

nodes.title: Applied Consumption Economics
nodes.description: Analysis and application of micro-economic principles as they relate to consumers, including consumption and saving, investments in human capital, market work, household production, and leisure choices. Pre/Co-requisite: CDAE 3540 or ECON 2450.
nodes.other_requirements: instructor permission

nodes.title: Elements of Statistics
nodes.description: Basic statistical concepts, methods, and applications, including correlation, regression, confidence intervals, and hypothesis tests. Prerequisites: Two years of high school algebra.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of high school algebra. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Communication Design I
nodes.description: Directed projects which explore the elements and principles of communication design. Design research, process, experimentation, and production in hand-based and computer-generated design application for multi-modal presentations. Prerequisite: Community Development & Applied Economics major or minor; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Community Development & Applied Economics major or minor; or Instructor permission; Applied Design Minor only, or instructor permission. Required materials for this course are a subscription to the Adobe Suite. Recommended prerequisite: CDAE 1150, Visual Communication

nodes.title: CL:Community Media Production
nodes.description: A hands-on media-based class in which students work collaboratively, producing one long-form documentary or many short-form videos about a local community member, issue, or campaign. Students produce media for entertainment, social media, and informational purposes and learn what community media is and how it can develop community. Prerequisite: CDAE 1240.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Media-Policy-Action
nodes.description: Examines the connections between media, public policy, and policy outcomes. Provides hands-on learning (action) experiences in news reporting and policy-making through the lens of the Vermont Legislature and Vermont's policy and media culture. Prerequisites: Any of the following: ENGL 1001, ENGL 1740, POLS 1300, CDAE 1020, CDAE 1150, CDAE 1240.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Basic Statistical Methods 1
nodes.description: Foundational course for students taking further quantitative courses. Exploratory data analysis, probability distributions, estimation, hypothesis testing. Introductory regression, experimentation, contingency tables, and nonparametrics. Computer software used. Credit not awarded for more than one of STAT 1410 or STAT 2430.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for more than one of STAT 1410 or STAT 2430. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: SL: Comm Develpmt: St Lucia II
nodes.description: Lectures or readings on contemporary issues in Community Development and Applied Economics. Enrollment may be more than once, up to twelve hours.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: SL:CommDevelpmt:St Lucia I
nodes.description: A general introduction to problems of sustainable development on small island developing states utilizing a case study of St. Lucia, West Indies. Prerequisites: CDAE 1020 or CDAE 1610; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Instructor permission. PACE student by Permission and Override. Students registered for CDAE 2860 are required to enroll in CDAE 2870 during the winter semester. Students are expected to have had previous coursework in economics, including at least one of the following: ECON 1400, ECON 1450, CDAE 1601, or similar. Students should have an introductory understanding of project management and community development.

nodes.title: Digital Illustration
nodes.description: Digital illustration introduces methods of conceptualizing and executing illustrations to solve communication problems, using a range of techniques within vector and raster-based software applications. Prerequisite: CDAE 1150 or ARTS 1400; Community Development & Applied Economics major or minor; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Community Development & Applied Economics major or minor; Instructor permission. Prereqs enforced by the system: CDAE Majors and Minors, ELPD Majors, or instructor permission; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Intr to Dev Carib & Cent Am
nodes.description: This interdisciplinary course introduces students to the culture, history, diversity, geography, and the impact of ethnicity, poverty, and oppression on development in the Caribbean and Central America.

nodes.title: The Real Cost of Food
nodes.description: Learn how producers, processors, wholesalers, cooperatives, retailers, consumers, and governments affect the movement of food and fiber products through the production-marketing chain. Prerequisite: CDAE 1610 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Applied Research Methods
nodes.description: Methods used in the collection and analysis of qualitative and quantitative data. Critical review of literature, and data collection, analysis, and interpretation for descriptive, inferential, and evaluation research. Prerequisites: One of the following: STAT 1410, STAT 1110, or equivalent course. Must register for CDAE 3500 lab.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for CDAE 3500 lab. Must register for a CDAE 3500 OL1 - OL3 lab; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Graphic Design
nodes.description: The application of graphic design principles to practical problems, including the impact of popular design on society, and the exploration of visual elements in contemporary printing processes. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, ARTH, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $45.00

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Consumer,Markets&Public Policy
nodes.description: Analysis of consumer choices through the examination of consumer behavior theories, current marketplace issues and public policy. Prerequisite: One of the following: CDAE 1240, CDAE 1150, ENGL 1001, ENGL 1740, or permission.
nodes.other_requirements: permission

nodes.title: Leadership in Practice
nodes.description: Study of leadership theory and how it informs the practice of leadership. Focus on applying leadership theory to personal practice learning how leadership affects the changes that organizations face. Prerequisite: CDAE 1240.
nodes.other_requirements: Asynchronous online course

nodes.title: US Political System
nodes.description: Institutions, processes, and problems of American government. Credit not awarded for both POLS 1300 and POLS 1013.

nodes.title: Marketing: Com Entrepreneurs
nodes.description: Marketing concepts and methods and their applications for community entrepreneurs. Focus on development of marketing plan and its use in guiding business operations. Prerequisite: CDAE 1610 or permission.
nodes.other_requirements: equivalent or instructor permission

nodes.title: Project Development & Planning
nodes.description: National, community, and private sector project development. Focus on planning methods and policy instruments, sectoral linkages, and contributions to the economy as a whole. Pre/co-requisites: CDAE 2020 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Prereqs enforced by the system

nodes.title: Event Planning for Athletics
nodes.description: Focuses on providing students basic knowledge and skills of event planning with real-life experiences. Topics covered include sponsorship, fundraising, marketing, promotions, branding, ticket operations, social media, event operations, and risk management in support of event production. Prerequisites: ENGL 1001, CDAE 1240, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Asynchronous online course

nodes.title: SL:Adv Sust DevSm IslandStates
nodes.description: This course is an advanced course in problems of sustainable development on small island developing states utilizing a case study of St. Lucia, West Indies. Prerequisites: CDAE 2860 and Instructor permission required.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required.

nodes.title: CL:Community Design Studio
nodes.description: Problem-based community design studio course with research on existing conditions, needs assessment, sense of place, and development of sustainable and integrative design solutions and processes. Prerequisites: CDAE 1150, CDAE 1010, or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: Principles of Comm Dev Econ
nodes.description: Introduction to principles of microeconomics and their application to food and agricultural markets, resource management, and community development.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Department permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Department permission. Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Inclusive ScienceCommunication
nodes.description: Science communication theories, contexts, and practices with a focus on inclusion, equity, and intersectionality. Students examine the relationship between science and society before developing written, visual, spoken, and mediated messages promoting respect and shared understandings of science among researchers, journalists, public relations specialists, policy officials, and the public. Prerequisite: CDAE 1240.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students. Requires attention to respect and shared understanding across expert fields. Attend class. Engage in class. Be ready for class. Submit assignments on time. Willingness to explore new ideas in a respectful manner. Create a safe space for all members of our class to feel included, valuable, and brave.

nodes.title: CL:Social MediaTheory2Practice
nodes.description: Explores social media from theoretical and professional practical perspectives, immersing students in the complex and multifaceted world of social media communication. Prerequisites: CDAE 1240, CDAE 1150, CALS 1020, or CALS 1850.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Foundation:Information Tech
nodes.description: Foundational course to acclimate College of Agriculture & Life Science First-Year students to college life and develop information technology skills through use of computer hardware and software and internet applications.
nodes.other_requirements: CALS First-Year, sophomore and PACE students only; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: CL:Intro Comm Entrepreneurship
nodes.description: Introduction to the theory and practice of developing and operating an entrepreneurial activity based on specific business. Emphasis on business development, operation, financing, marketing, and social responsibility. Prerequisites: One of the following: CDAE 1020, CDAE 1610, or permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; Instructor Permission; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: IS:Territories of Life
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Microeconomics for Appl Econ
nodes.description: The study of economic choices of individuals and firms, and the analysis of competitive and noncompetitive markets. Emphasis on application of intermediate microeconomic theory. Prerequisites: CDAE 2020 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Computer Applications
nodes.description: Use of computer operating systems programming languages, electronic communications, word processing, spreadsheet modeling and graphics, and internet software related to the agricultural and life sciences.

nodes.title: Entrepreneurial Mindset
nodes.description: For students curious about entrepreneurship. Examines the entrepreneurial mindset - characteristics and competencies of entrepreneurs; explores entrepreneurship in all types of organizations and how the entrepreneurial mindset can support the success of any venture.

nodes.title: Community Org & Development
nodes.description: The roles of forms of community capital, civic engagement, leadership, social and political institutions, and communities of place and interest in a community development context. Pre/co-requisites: Junior standing; CDAE 2020 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior standing

nodes.title: Foundations:Communication Meth
nodes.description: Foundational course to acclimate College of Agriculture & Life Science First-Year students to college life and develop individual and group public speaking skills through giving and critically analyzing presentations.

nodes.title: Consumer Law and Policy
nodes.description: Law as an expression of public policy to protect consumers in the marketplace. Emphasis on laws prohibiting deceptive advertising and marketing practices. Prerequisites: ENGL 1001, ENGL 1210, CDAE 1240, or CDAE 1610; Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Sophomore standing

nodes.title: SL:Strategic Writing for PCOM
nodes.description: Students learn to write standard messages and documents including e-mail, memos, letters to the editor, fundraising letters, news releases, brochures, and feature stories. Prerequisites: CDAE 1240, ENGL 1001, or ENGL 1740; Public Communication majors/minors only.
nodes.other_requirements: Public Communication majors/minors only.

nodes.title: Research & Evaluation Methods
nodes.description: Conceptualization, collection and analysis of primary and secondary data; interpretation, and communication of results of applied research and/or evaluation studies for decision makers. Separate lab required. Prerequisite: Three hours of Statistics. Cross-listed with: PA 6030.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours of Statistics. Must register for lab CDAE 6510 L01; Cross listed with PA 6030; Total combined enrollment: 25; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Propaganda, Media, & Cit Respn
nodes.description: Develops critical thinking skills about news media. Studies propaganda, media ownership, and the use of print media, radio, television, and the internet, to influence the public through various propaganda techniques from 1900 to present. Prerequisite: CDAE 1150 or CDAE 1240 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: From Big Bang to the Ecozoic
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Ecological Economics Theory
nodes.description: Lectures or readings on contemporary issues in Community Development & Applied Economics.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate Students Only; excellent undergraduate students can take it with instructor permission.

nodes.title: CL:Design Innovation II
nodes.description: Emphasizes the human-processes for successful design innovation across myriad real-world contexts rather than design innovation within any one context area. Broad range of design applications/interests will include but not be limited to: Universal Design, Communication Design, Landscape/Architectural Design, Ecological/Environmental Design, and Community Planning, Urban+Rural Design. Prerequisite: CDAE 1600.

nodes.title: Communication Design II
nodes.description: Explores visual communication through advanced projects in design research, planning, iteration, technical and software experimentation, and production for multi-modal design applications. Prerequisite: CDAE 1160.

nodes.title: Dec Making:Comm Entrepreneurs
nodes.description: Quantitative decision-making methods and applications for community entrepreneurs. Major topics include linear programming, risk and uncertainty, inventory decisions, and e-commerce. Prerequisites: CDAE 2660, MATH 1212, and CALS 1850 or CALS 1020.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereqs enforced by the system:Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: Communication Law
nodes.description: Legal issues in mass media, including: freedom of speech, libel, invasion of privacy, obscenity and indecency, copyright and trademark. Prerequisite: CDAE 1240.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to limits and differential/integral calculus with a wide variety of applications. Students interested in intensive use of mathematics should take MATH 1234. Credit not awarded for both MATH 1212 and MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248. See MATH 1242. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system: MATH 1012 or MATH 1034 or ALEKS MPA of at least 61; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Sports Media
nodes.description: A hands-on video production class broken into three sections: sporting event coverage working with CATAMOUNT TV, sports journalism collaborating with The Vermont Cynic, and contributions scaffolding into a sports documentary with ESPN's 30 - FOR - 30 as a model. Prerequisites: ENGL 1001, CDAE 1240, or EDPE 3200.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Small Group Communication
nodes.description: An introduction to small group communication theories, research, and skills. Discussion and group activities focus on communication that fosters effective, creative, inclusive, and transdisciplinary teaming in a variety of practical situations and community-based contexts.

nodes.title: Socially Responsible Marketing
nodes.description: Addresses communication with the public to build stronger, healthier, safer communities. Students use public communication skills to craft messages for a defined audience and consider how public/private entrepreneurs/organizations can help solve societal problems, particularly related to college-aged audiences.

nodes.title: AI & Society
nodes.description: Interdisciplinary seminar that explores the social, cultural, and ethical dimensions of Artificial Intelligence and examines AI's impact on such topics as health, the environment, work/economy, and relationships- highlighting both its benefits and harms. Through discussion and debate, students consider tensions between technological progress and human well-being. Also investigates AI's digital infrastructure, corporate influence, and community-driven design, encouraging critical reflection on agency, values, and the role of technology in shaping society. Prerequisites: CALS 1010, CALS 1020, CDAE 1020, CDAE 1240, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Design Innovation I
nodes.description: Design is essential to creating innovative, useful, and effective solutions to meet complex real-world needs. Design Innovation I offers an introduction to design theories and processes, understanding historic and contemporary contributions, and exploring applications across various fields of practice.

nodes.title: Drafting Design II
nodes.description: Using a computer to create, manipulate, and record drafting and design concepts, symbols, and conventions to prepare technical and/or presentation drawings at the intermediate level. Students will learn in applied context relating to real world needs. Prerequisite: CDAE 1010 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereqs enforced by the system

nodes.title: Solutions in Enviro Studies
nodes.description: Analysis and critique of grand challenges in environmental studies with an emphasis on understanding and solving pervasive global and local environmental problems such as global climate change.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for an ENVS 1510 A01 - A03 discussion section; Fee: $15.00; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Privacy Law & Policy
nodes.description: Privacy and Data Security have become increasingly prominent issues in recent years. Taught by a practicing consumer protection attorney, students will learn the role privacy plays in society, both now and historically, identify and interpret the domestic and internal laws that protect privacy, understand global privacy regimes, and engage in the ongoing privacy debate and prepare for future privacy concerns. Prerequisites: CDAE 1020, CDAE 1240, CDAE 1400, OR CDAE 1610. Cross-listed with: CIS 2330.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: SL:Comm Develpmt:St Lucia II
nodes.description: The travel component to CDAE 2860. Prerequisite: CDAE 2860.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. Students registered for CDAE 2860 are required to enroll in CDAE 2870 during the winter semester. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Microeconomic Theory
nodes.description: Analysis of consumer demand, supply, market price under competitive conditions and monopolistic influences, and the theory of income distribution. Prerequisites: ECON 1400, ECON 1450, MATH 1212 or MATH 1234; or Health & Society major or minor, Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Health & Society major or minor, Instructor permission. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate standing only

nodes.title: Macroeconomics for Appl Econ
nodes.description: Explore macroeconomic principles and concepts as they affect individuals and businesses in local, regional, national, and global economics. Prerequisites: CDAE 2020 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: SL:Consumer Law in Action II
nodes.description: Practicum providing experience working as an advanced consumer advocate in the Consumer Assistance Program office. Builds on CDAE 2590 experience with students addressing more complex consumer complaints and inquiries as well as leading student teams. Jointly sponsored with the Vermont Attorney General's Office. Prerequisites: CDAE 2590 and Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Minimum Sophomore Standing; Contact instructor for location

nodes.title: Community Economic Development
nodes.description: Examines how rural and urban communities address poverty, unemployment and other economic problems through job creation and retention, workforce training and support, and other development strategies. Cross-listed with: PA 6260.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate standing only

nodes.title: Contemp Policy Iss:Comm Dev
nodes.description: In-depth study of sustainable development policy issues, with emphasis on understanding systematic interactions among economic development, biodiversity conservation, climate change, energy, food and watershed planning. Prerequisites: One of the following: CDAE 2020, CDAE 2710, CDAE 2860, or equivalent course.
nodes.other_requirements: equivalent course

nodes.title: Strategic Communication
nodes.description: Examination of strategic communication and how it impacts consumers and the economy. Extensive application of critical analysis to actual strategic communication campaigns from development through evaluation including advertising and other consumer-related ends. Prerequisites: CDAE 1150 or CDAE 1240.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Fund of Public Communication
nodes.description: Provides the foundation for understanding communication components, processes, contexts, and applications and introduces research and theory through critique and case study.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Intro to Envrnmtl Studies
nodes.description: Survey of the interdisciplinary field of environmental studies. Examines ecological, cultural, historical, and political influences on the role of humans in local and global ecosystems and the grand challenges humanity faces. Required for Environmental Studies majors and minors.

nodes.title: Publication Design
nodes.description: Focuses on strategic development of multipage composition for print and digital formats, creation and selection of imagery and the effective organization of content and visual style to make information accessible, visually engaging, and readable for the intended audience. Prerequisites: CDAE 1150, CDAE 1170 or CDAE 1160, Public Communication major or Applied Design Minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Public Communication major or Applied Design Minor. Open to COM Majors and Applied Design Minors only, or instructor permission. Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Strat PlanCommEntrepreneurs
nodes.description: Applications of marketing, finance, and management strategies. Drafting a real working business plan for community entrepreneurs and economic development. Prerequisites: One of the following: CDAE 2660, CDAE 2670, CDAE 2680, or equivalent course; Senior standing only.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing only. CENT majors & minors with Senior standing and PACE students; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Independent Study L4E
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Written Expression
nodes.description: A foundational composition course featuring a sequence of writing, reading, and information literacy assignments. Students learn to write and revise for different rhetorical situations while increasing their mastery of academic conventions. Some sections designed for specific student audiences.

nodes.title: Perspectives on Art Making
nodes.description: Introduction to contemporary art practice in various media. Explores method and meaning in art making, the role of experimentation, and the translation of experience into artwork. Credit not awarded for both ARTS 1400 and ARTS 1014.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed.

nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research for the Doctoral Dissertation.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate standing only

nodes.title: Principles of Microeconomics
nodes.description: Study of individual economic units with particular emphasis on market interactions among firms and households.

nodes.title: Global Media & Intl Developmen
nodes.description: Focuses on an understanding of global communication issues related to international development. Examines different aspects of global communication, such as world press systems, codes of ethics, new world information and communication order, cultural imperialism and public diplomacy. Prerequisite: CDAE 1020, CDAE 1240, CDAE 1610, or ENVS 1510.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Visual Communication
nodes.description: Introduction and analysis of aesthetics and function of design in the context of communications and marketing, the built environment, and community development.

nodes.title: Agricultural Policy and Ethics
nodes.description: An examination of American agriculture and policies from various perspectives - historical, political, ecological, technological, social, economic, and ethical. Emphasis on contemporary issues, policy options, and future development. Prerequisites: CDAE 2020 or PSS 3120 or equivalent. Cross-listed with: ALE 3080.
nodes.other_requirements: equivalent

nodes.title: US Food, Social Equity &Dev
nodes.description: Provides an introduction to gender, race, class, and ethnicity with particular emphasis on food, population, economic, and ecological issues in sustainable agriculture, food systems, and community development. The geographical focus emphasizes the United States.

nodes.title: Energy Alternatives
nodes.description: Concepts of energy, work, and power. Energy conversion, utilization, and conservation. Alternatives to fossil fuels including solar, wind, biomass, etc. Energy systems for rural areas.

nodes.title: Principles of Macroeconomics
nodes.description: Introduction to economic concepts, institutions, and analysis, particularly as related to the economy as a whole. May be taught with traditional approach or with strong mathematical emphasis.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students. Knowledge of high school algebra.

nodes.title: World Food,Pop & Develop
nodes.description: Agricultural development emphasizing natural and economic phenomena and the effect of food supplies on population trends and policies.

nodes.title: Fin Mgmt: Comm Entrepreneurs
nodes.description: Understanding and creating business and personal financial records for entrepreneurs including applications common to entrepreneurial business practices using contemporary financial software. Prerequisite: CDAE 2660 or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Total credit toward graduation in CDAE 2991 and CDAE 3991 cannot exceed 15 credits. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; Must have a plan on file; S/U Grading Only; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Public Communication Media
nodes.description: Students gain insight into mass media and contemporary issues, social marketing with local Service Learning agency partners, social polling, and the interaction of media, governance, law, and ethics. Prerequisite: CDAE 1240.
nodes.other_requirements: Sophomore Standing; COM majors or minors only

nodes.title: The Art of the Essay
nodes.description: In this intermediate writing course, students explore and practice variations in the genre known as the nonfiction essay, attending to audience, purpose, context, style, and medium.

nodes.title: SL:Public Comm Capstone
nodes.description: Students work with non-profit and municipal community partners to develop professional level communications strategies and materials. Students complete their professional public communication portfolios and resumes. Prerequisites: Senior standing; CDAE 1240, CDAE 1150, and CDAE 2210 or CDAE 2200.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing; COM majors with Senior Standing; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing. Instructor permission required; Minimum Junior standing PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Intro to Emergency Management
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Communicating Climate Crises
nodes.description: Examines efforts to emphasize and elevate the importance of addressing the existential threat posed by climate change while exploring other potential approaches to communicating climate change. Covers: 1) identifying the challenges posed in communicating climate change 2) exploring foundations for thinking about the relationship between communication, environment, and experience 3) developing a repertoire for connecting people with climate through communicative action. Prerequisites: CDAE 1240 or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: SDPEG Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Protect Your Privacy
nodes.description: Every detail about individuals' lives is shared, bought, sold, monetized, and sometimes stolen. This practical course explores privacy threats, legal protections, and tools that exist to protect privacy.

nodes.title: SL:Consumer Law in Action I
nodes.description: Under supervision of an attorney, students respond to real-world phone, online, and mail requests for consumer information and handle consumer complaints to connect consumers with appropriate and effective resources, professionals, and protections. Sponsored with the Vermont Attorney General's Office. Prerequisite: CDAE 2570 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Sophomore standing

nodes.title: Career Builder: LinkedIn
nodes.description: Learn how to use LinkedIn to build or strengthen your professional profile, find your next job or internship, and grow your professional network. Develop communication competence and confidence relevant to your unique experiences and career goals.

nodes.title: Activist Journalism
nodes.description: Students research and produce multimedia news stories that promote a social justice cause of their choice. Using traditional journalistic approaches and new digital media tools, students will investigate, publish, and distribute stories with a local, national, and global impact. Prerequisites: CDAE 1240 or ENVS 1500 or ENGL 1001 or FWIL Course or TAP Course; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: FWIL Course or TAP Course; minimum Sophomore standing.

nodes.title: Sport in Society
nodes.description: Examines sport as a social institution, emphasizing interrelationships between sport and the social context in which it exists; analyzes functions and dysfunctions of sport in contemporary society.

nodes.title: Risk& Resilience Communication
nodes.description: Lectures or readings on contemporary issues in Community Development and Applied Economics. Enrollment may be more than once, up to twelve hours.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Drafting Design I
nodes.description: Creating pictorial presentation and 3D model drawings using computer software. Basic methods and procedures of architectural, three-view, oblique, isometric, and perspective computer-aided drawings.

nodes.title: News Writing Across Media
nodes.description: Students learn to report and write news for print, online, and broadcast formats through practical application of media literacy skills and study of current events. Prerequisite: ENGL 1001 or ENGL 1740.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Tolkien:Gender/Race, the Other
nodes.description: Introductory courses addressing the representation and construction of race in literature and/or the contributions of ethnically diverse writers to the American culture. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Cross-listed with: CRES 1821.
